trade.summary: Italy and South Africa maintain a solid trade relationship. Italy exports machinery, vehicles, chemicals, and fashion goods; South Africa exports minerals, metals, agricultural products, and chemicals. South Africa is Italy's most important sub-Saharan Africa trading partner. Italy's Mattei Plan for Africa elevates South Africa's importance as a partner.

diplomatic.summary: Italy and South Africa maintain diplomatic relations with periodic high-level contacts. Both are G20 members, providing a framework for dialogue. South Africa's non-aligned foreign policy and its positions on Ukraine and Gaza can diverge from Italy's EU-coordinated stances. Italy's Mattei Plan for Africa explicitly targets sub-Saharan Africa including South Africa as an energy and development partner.
recent_incidents: Divergence on Gaza (South Africa strongly anti-Israel)

military.summary: Limited military ties. Some Italian defense equipment sales to South Africa historically. No formal alliance. Italy and South Africa cooperate in some UN peacekeeping contexts in Africa.

financial.summary: Moderate bilateral investment. Italian companies have operations in South Africa in mining, manufacturing, and retail. ENI and other Italian energy firms have potential interest in South Africa's gas and renewable energy sector. South African investment in Italy is limited.

energy.summary: Growing energy partnership. Italy is interested in South African natural gas and renewable energy exports as part of the Mattei Plan and energy diversification strategy. ENI is exploring South African offshore gas. South Africa-Italy energy cooperation is a strategic Italian priority.

scientific.summary: Moderate scientific cooperation. Joint research in marine science, archaeology (Italy has significant interest in African heritage sites), and earth sciences. Italian universities have exchange programs with top South African institutions (UCT, Wits).
